findings.title: Duplicated Produce Findings boilerplate across 10 scan skills
findings.description: An 18-line 'Compile findings array' instruction block with JSON example template appears in all 10 scan-* skills. Each also includes the identical lines 'Always populate snippet with the relevant code lines' and 'Return the findings array to the orchestrator'. This constitutes a repeated instruction block exceeding the 5-line threshold across 10 skills.
findings.recommendation: Extract the Produce Findings boilerplate into a shared reference document (e.g., references/findings-template.md) and reference it from each scan skill, similar to how scanner-protocol.md is already referenced.

findings.title: Duplicated Scanner Protocol reference across 10 scan skills
findings.description: All 10 scan skills contain a '## Scanner Protocol' section with identical text (verified: 1 unique variant across 10 files) instructing the reader to read scanner-protocol.md. This 6-line section is copy-pasted verbatim — only the section heading is reproduced, the body is completely identical.
findings.recommendation: Consider whether the code-analyzer agent could inject this reference automatically, removing the need for each skill to declare it. Alternatively, accept this as intentional redundancy for skill self-containment.

findings.title: Duplicated Success Checklist pattern across 13 skills
findings.description: 13 of 18 skills include a '## Success Checklist' section with a markdown checkbox list. While checklist content is skill-specific, the structural framing is duplicated across all of them.
findings.recommendation: The Success Checklist is a useful self-validation tool for each skill. The duplication is structural rather than content-based. Low-priority to address.

findings.title: Stale deprecated output-schemas.md references in 15 skill files
findings.snippet: Compile findings array with each finding matching the Finding schema from `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/references/output-schemas.md`:
findings.description: 15 skill files reference `output-schemas.md` in their 'Produce Findings' steps (e.g., scan-quality, scan-security, critique-plan, generate-refactoring-plan, reconcile-report, etc.). The file `references/output-schemas.md` is marked DEPRECATED as of v0.8.0 and its header instructs agents to use split schema fragments from `references/schemas/` instead. The code-analyzer AGENT.md correctly loads `finding-schema.md` and `scoring-schema.md`, but the skill bodies haven't been updated to match. This creates ambiguous guidance: the agent protocol says to load split schemas, but the skill workflow text still points to the deprecated monolith.
findings.recommendation: Update all 15 affected skill files to reference the appropriate split schema file: scan-* skills should reference `references/schemas/finding-schema.md`, critique skills should reference `references/schemas/critic-schema.md`, and plan-generator skills should reference `references/schemas/plan-schema.md`. Remove the deprecated pointer.

findings.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy in scan-quality SKILL.md
findings.snippet:
### Step 1 — Map Source Files
...
### Sub-Section: Tech Debt
#### Step 7 — Grep for TODO Markers
...
### Sub-Section: Performance
#### Step 11 — Scan Data Access Patterns
...
### Step 16 — Produce Findings
findings.description: Steps 1–6 are at H3 level (### Step N) directly under ## Workflow, while Steps 7–15 are at H4 level (#### Step N) nested under H3 'Sub-Section' groupings (Tech Debt, Performance). Step 16 returns to H3. This creates an inconsistent step numbering depth where some steps are peers and others are children.
findings.recommendation: Either promote Steps 7–15 to H3 like Steps 1–6 (removing or repurposing the Sub-Section groupings), or demote Steps 1–6 under their own H3 grouping (e.g., '### Sub-Section: Code Health').

findings.title: Large inline JSON example blocks in Produce Findings sections
findings.description: Each of the 10 scan skills contains a 15-line JSON example block inside the Produce Findings section. The examples are dimension-specific (different ID prefixes, titles, descriptions) but follow the same schema structure. The examples consume significant word count in each skill.
findings.recommendation: Reference finding-schema.md for the schema definition and provide only a 3-4 line abbreviated example in each skill, or move full examples to a shared reference with per-dimension variants.
